source.title: Goals and habits in the brain

confidence_rationale: Dolan & Dayan's comprehensive review of goal-directed vs habitual decision-making provides the theoretical scaffolding that situates ARC-018's hippocampal viability map. They articulate the model-based/model-free distinction, clarify that goal-directed behaviour requires a cognitive map whose value is updated prospectively (not merely retrospectively), and review the evidence that hippocampus is an essential substrate for model-based planning. This is theoretical grounding rather than direct experimental evidence, hence the lower confidence -- but the conceptual alignment is high. The review's framing of 'model-based' as requiring explicit forward simulation is exactly what ARC-018 claims for the viability map. Confidence is limited by the fact that ARC-018 makes specific mechanistic claims (E2 indexing, E3 error update) that go well beyond what this review can address.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Goal-directed behaviour requires a model of the world that supports prospective simulation of action consequences; hippocampus is central to model-based planning; the goal-directed/habitual spectrum maps onto model-based/model-free RL; flexible value updating (e.g. after reward revaluation) depends on model-based representations.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-018's viability map is the specific hippocampal structure that enables model-based planning. Dolan & Dayan provide the theoretical foundation: the map must encode transition structure and outcome values in a way that permits prospective simulation and flexible updating. The paper's identification of hippocampus as the locus of model-based representation supports ARC-018's claim; the emphasis on flexible updating under new goal/harm information directly supports the E3-error-driven update component.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is a review of behavioural and lesion literature; it does not address the specific indexing coordinate (spatial vs action-object) or the E3 error signal mechanism. The claim that hippocampus is the model-based substrate is well-supported but not universally agreed -- some accounts distribute this role across prefrontal cortex and hippocampus.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review, rodent and human literature, behavioural and lesion studies.

confidence_components.notes: High-quality theoretical source, but mapping fidelity is moderate because the review does not address the V3-specific claims (action-object indexing, E3 error signals). Works mainly as theoretical grounding for the viability-map framing.
